MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-26-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 502

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 502, DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP), NO APPEARANCES, NOT CONTESTED, AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE APPLICATION OF PLAINTIFF GARY HOWDYSHELL ("PLAINTIFF") FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANTS (1) KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC., IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,087,500.00 AND (2) K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC., IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,087,500.00 IS GRANTED. FOLLOWING THE HEARING, THE JUDICIAL OFFICER SIGNED THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT. JUDGE: VICTOR M. HWANG. CLERK: EDWARD SANTOS. BAILIFF: NOT PRESENT. NOT REPORTED. (502)

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Continued To Dec-03-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 502

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar May-26-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances, Not contested, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

The Application of Plaintiff Gary Howdyshell ("Plaintiff") for Entry of Default Judgment against Defendants (1) Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc., in the amount of $2,087,500.00 and (2) K-M Industries Holding Co., Inc., in the amount of $2,087,500.00 is GRANTED.

The Court has reviewed the prove-up packet including the Judicial Council Form CIV-100, the memorandum, Mr. Nevin's declaration and the attached exhibits, which are admitted. The Court finds Defendants have been served with process, failed to answer or appear and defend the action within the time allowed by law, and default has been duly entered. As such, Plaintiffs are entitled to the relief sought in the Application. The Court intends to execute the proposed judgment accompanying the application.

Following the hearing, the Judicial Officer signed the Default Judgment.

Judge: Victor M. Hwang. Clerk: Edward Santos. Bailiff: Not Present. Not Reported. (502)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Continued To Mar-23-26 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2025, in Department 304, Line
Steve Patti, ESQ of Brayton Purcell LLP for Plaintiff, No appearance from Counsel for Defendant, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Mead Clark Lumber Company's ("Defendant" or "Mead Clark") Motion for Summary Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Opposition filed. Reply filed.
Defendant failed to give sufficient notice pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure
section 437c(a)(2). Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2) provides that "notice of the motion and supporting papers shall be served on all other parties to the action at least 81 days before the time appointed for hearing." If the notice is served electronically, the required 81-day period of notice must be increased by two court days. (Code Civ. Proc., 437c(a)(2) and 1010.6(a)(4)(B); see Cole v. Superior Court (2022) 87 Cal.App.5th 84, 87-88.) The Court cannot shorten the notice period without the parties' prior consent. (Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 764; McMahon v. Super. Ct. (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 117-118.) Nor can the Court cure the defect by continuing the hearing by the missing number of days. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267-1268.) The notice period is mandatory. (Robinson v. Woods (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1267-68.)
Defendant filed this motion for summary judgment on May 30, 2025, 81 days prior to the hearing date of August 19, 2025. The proof of service shows the motion was e-served on the same date. However, because Defendant chose to serve the motion electronically, it was required to serve the motion two court days earlier, by May 28, 2025. Defendant's motion was therefore two days late.
In reply, Defendant states that the parties are in the process of finalizing a stipulation to continue the trial date and intend to appear ex parte and request the continuance. (Reply, 1.) Based on the anticipated trial continuance, Defendant request that the hearing on its motion be continued to track the new trial date. (Id.) The Court declines to do so. The statutory notice requirements under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2) remain in effect and Defendant's failure to comply with those requirements is not excused by a possibility of a future trial date change.
According, Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment must therefore be taken off calendar.
Order signed in open court. =(EPS/304).

DEFENDANT MEAD CLARK LUMBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - FOR TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY
